title: Murtughas piriformis
taxonomy: Murtughas piriformis is a plant species that belongs to the family of flowering plants, specifically within the order of Apiales. It is a type of flowering plant that is native to certain regions. The classification of Murtughas piriformis is further detailed as being within the genus Murtughas and the family Pittosporaceae. The plant is often associated with the classification of being part of the kingdom Plantae. Further research would be needed to confirm the exact classification of this plant species.

Its medicinal properties are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and to confirm its efficacy in treating various ailments. Some traditional remedies suggest that Murtughas piriformis can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Further studies are required to validate its medicinal uses and to explore its full potential as a natural remedy.

The bioactive constituents of Murraya koenigii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
